summary: Backend regression test for Bugs 2, 5, 6, 8, 14. Auth flow, onboarding with all fields, goal-specific exercises, bodyweight, and settings save flow tested with two fresh users (strongman + hypertrophy). 20/24 tests passed. 2 real backend bugs found, 2 are RAG behavior edge cases.

critical.issue: Returns 404 'Profile not found for this user' for newly registered users. Root cause: POST /api/profile/intake only calls db.profile.update_one if existing_profile is found (line 2016-2018), never upserts. New users therefore have no db.profile document. Fix: change to upsert=True or use update_one with upsert.

Returns 0 entries for new users after intake. Change-log reads from db.substitutions (written only by apply-injury-update), but apply-injury-update fails for new users → no entries ever written. Also, intake events are logged to in-memory _prog_store['changes'] which the change-log endpoint does NOT read. Change-log will always show 0 for new users until apply-injury-update is fixed.

minor.issue: Strongman with log equipment + Shoulder injury → Shoulder injury blocks Log Press AND Incline Bench, resulting in Incline Bench as fallback (it's also blocked). Both strongman and hypertrophy end up with 'Incline Bench' as ME_UPPER main when Shoulder injury is present. The fallback in _build_me_upper for strongman doesn't check if the fallback 'Incline Bench' is also blocked.

action_items: CRITICAL (Bug 8): Fix POST /api/profile/intake to upsert db.profile instead of update-only. In server.py ~line 2016: change `if existing_profile: db.profile.update_one(...)` to `db.profile.update_one({'userId': user_id}, {'$set': profile_update}, upsert=True)`. This will also fix change-log being empty.

MINOR: In plan_generator.py _build_me_upper, strongman fallback 'Incline Bench' is itself blocked by Shoulder injury. Add a final safety fallback that checks blocked set before assigning, e.g. use 'Overhead Press' or 'DB Press' that's not in blocked list.
NOTE (Bug 2 CONFIRMED FIXED): JWT userId correctly used (not DEFAULT_USER 'user_001'). profile.userId returns the actual auth user UUID.
NOTE (Bug 6 CONFIRMED FIXED): All fields including primaryWeaknesses, specialtyEquipment, sleepHours, stressLevel accepted by intake endpoint.
NOTE (Bug 14 CONFIRMED FIXED): bodyweight=220 stored and returned in profile.bodyweight.

NOTE (Bug 5 PARTIALLY FIXED): ME Lower is correctly goal-specific (Yoke Walk for strongman vs Sumo Deadlift for hypertrophy via RAG). ME Upper both get Incline Bench when Shoulder injury blocks all pressing variants — this is edge case overlap, not a full bug.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py ~line 2016: intake endpoint uses if/update pattern instead of upsert for db.profile — new users never get a profile document written

plan_generator.py _build_me_upper: Strongman fallback to 'Incline Bench' doesn't verify it's not in blocked_upper set. Incline Bench IS blocked by Shoulder injury, leading to blocked exercise being selected anyway
seed_data_creation: Registered test_strongman@test.com and test_hypertrophy@test.com and ran intake for both.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Bug 8 (settings save flow) is NOT fully fixed. apply-injury-update returns 404 for new users because intake doesn't upsert db.profile. After E1 fixes the upsert issue, rerun TestSettingsSaveFlow tests. Bug 2/6/14 are confirmed fixed. Bug 5 is mostly fixed - RAG generates goal-different plans but when Shoulder injury is present both strongman+hypertrophy ME Upper converge on Incline Bench.
rca_of_issue: Bug 8 RCA: POST /api/profile/intake (server.py ~L2016) uses `find_one` + conditional `update_one` pattern. For new users, `find_one` returns None, so the `if existing_profile:` block is skipped and db.profile is never populated. Then apply-injury-update reads `db.profile.find_one` → None → raises 404. Fix: use `update_one({userId}, {$set: ...}, upsert=True)` to create profile if missing.
